question: When Thomas says ‘My Lord and my God,’ what makes this more than a startled outburst—and how does John frame it as the intended endpoint of the whole book?

choices.A: John presents it as a climactic confession followed by the book’s purpose statement, making it programmatic rather than accidental speech.
choices.B: Thomas is swearing and John records it as a mistake.
choices.C: Thomas is addressing the Father, not Jesus.
choices.D: John immediately corrects Thomas for idolatry.

teaching.title: The Gospel’s climactic confession
teaching.body: Thomas’ confession is positioned at the narrative summit, and John immediately anchors it to the stated purpose: that readers believe Jesus is the Christ and Son of God. The placement signals intention: Thomas says out loud what John wants you to conclude about Jesus’ identity.
